sections.title: SCOPE
sections.text: This standard applies to the fish oils described in Section 2 that are presented in a state for human consumption. For the purpose of this standard, the term fish oils refers to oils derived from fish and shellfish as defined in Section 2 of the Code of Practice for Fish and Fishery Products (CXC 52-2003). This standard only applies to fish oils used in food and in food supplements where those are regulated as foods.

page_spans.text: This standard applies to the fish oils described in Section 2 that are presented in a state for human consumption. For the purpose of this standard, the term fish oils refers to oils derived from fish and shellfish as defined in Section 2 of the Code of Practice for Fish and Fishery Products (CXC 52-2003). This standard only applies to fish oils used in food and in food supplements where those are regulated as foods.

sections.title: DESCRIPTION
sections.text: Fish oils means oils intended for human consumption derived from the raw material as defined in Section 2 of CXC 52-2003. Processes to obtain fish oil for human consumption may involve, but are not limited to, extraction of crude oil from raw material and refining of that crude oil. Fish oils and concentrated fish oils are primarily composed of glycerides of fatty acids whereas concentrated fish oils ethyl esters are primarily composed of fatty acids ethyl esters. Fish oils may contain other lipids and unsaponifiable constituents naturally present. Crude fish oils and crude fish liver oils are oils intended for human consumption after they have undergone further processing, e.g. refining and purification and have to comply with Section 3.1, as applicable, as well as with Sections 4, 6.1 and 7. Fish oils intended for direct human consumption shall comply with all sections of this standard. The refined fish oil production process typically includes several steps such as repeated heating at high temperatures as well as alkali/acid treatments and repeated removal of the water phase. Fish oils may also be subjected to processing steps (e.g. solvent extraction, saponification, re-esterification, trans-esterification).

sections.text: Antioxidants, sequestrants, antifoaming agents, and emulsifiers used in accordance with Table 1 and Table 2 of the General Standard for Food Additives (CXS 192-1995),3 in food category 02.1.3 (Lard, tallow, fish oil, and other animal fats) are acceptable for use in foods conforming to this standard. The flavourings used in products covered by this standard should comply with the Guidelines for the Use of Flavourings (CXG 66-2008).4

sections.title: Other essential compositional criteria
sections.text: For oil from Engraulis ringens (2.1.1) the sum of EPA and DHA has to be at least 27 percent (expressed as percentage of total fatty acids). For krill oils (2.1.3) the content of phospholipids shall be at least 30 w/w percent. Concentrated fish oils (2.5.1) and highly concentrated fish oils (2.5.2) shall contain at least 50 w/w percent of fatty acids as sum of EPA and DHA in the form of triglycerides and/or phospholipids.

sections.title: CONTAMINANTS
sections.text:
The products covered by this standard shall comply with the maximum levels of the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995).
The products covered by this standard shall comply with the maximum residue limits for pesticides and/or veterinary drugs established by the Codex Alimentarius Commission.

sections.text: It is recommended that the products covered by the provisions of this standard be prepared and handled in accordance with the appropriate sections of the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969), the Code of Practice for Fish and Fishery Products (CXC 52-2003), and the Code of Hygienic Practice for the Storage and Transport of Edible Fats and Oils in Bulk (CXC 36-1987).

sections.title: Labelling on non-retail containers
sections.text:
Information on the above labelling requirements shall be given either on the container or in accompanying documents, except that the name of the food, lot identification and the name and address of the manufacturer or packer shall appear on the container.
However, lot identification and the name and address of the manufacturer or packer may be replaced by an identification mark, provided that such a mark is clearly identifiable with the accompanying documents.
For crude fish oils and crude fish liver oils , the label shall indicate that these oils are intended for human consumption only after they have undergone further processing.

sections.title: Other labelling requirements
sections.text:
For fish liver oils (Sections  2.3 and 2.4) the content in vitamin A and vitamin D, naturally present or restored, shall be given if required by country of retail sale.
For all fish oils covered by this standard the content of EPA and DHA shall be given if required by country of retail sale.

pages.summary: A página contém o título principal do documento e um cabeçalho repetitivo. Há um texto interno relacionado ao título principal, sem subseções detectadas.
A página contém um cabeçalho repetitivo com código e numeração. Não há títulos ou seções detectadas nesta página, apenas texto editorial correção associado ao documento.

A página contém dois títulos principais: 1. SCOPE e 2. DESCRIPTION. O título 2 contém várias subseções numeradas de 2.1 a 2.6, com níveis adicionais conforme a numeração (ex: 2.1.1, 2.3.1, 2.5.1, etc.). O texto associado corresponde ao conteúdo explicativo de cada seção e subseção. Não há indicação clara de continuidade entre páginas. O cabeçalho da página contém o código e a numeração 'CXS 329-2017  3', que foi excluído do texto dos nós.

A página contém a seção principal 3 ESSENTIAL COMPOSITION AND QUALITY FACTORS com subseções 3.1 a 3.4 e seus textos correspondentes, e a seção principal 4 FOOD ADDITIVES. A seção 3 continua na próxima página, indicada pela flag continues_on_next_page=true. Textos estão associados aos nós corretos respeitando a ruptura natural entre subseções. O cabeçalho da página é preservado. Não há indicação de continuidade da seção 3 a partir da página anterior.

A página contém seções principais 5 a 8 com suas subseções em 6. A hierarquia segue numeração padrão e títulos em caixa alta. O texto está associado corretamente aos seus respectivos títulos. Não há continuidade de seção da página anterior nesta página. Todas as seções estão completas nesta página sem indicação de continuidade para a próxima página.

A página contém um único elemento estrutural principal que é uma tabela detalhada sobre composição de ácidos graxos de diferentes tipos de óleo de peixe, com muitos dados tabulares e legendas. Não há título formal numerado, mas a tabela é claramente o foco da página. Não há indícios de que o conteúdo continua de outras páginas ou continuará na próxima página.

A página contém um único título de nível 1 sem numeração, "NOTES", que funciona como o cabeçalho da seção. Todo o texto subsequente consiste em referências numeradas relacionadas, associadas à seção NOTES. Não há subtítulos ou outras divisões hierárquicas. O cabeçalho repetitivo "CXS 329-2017  7" foi identificado e excluído do texto dos nós. O conteúdo não continua de página anterior nem segue para a próxima.
